  transport resolution while keeping the legacy transport interface

  runtime snapshot materialization, and project conversation runtime
  flows onto execution surfaces instead of websocket mode checks
- update machine transport tests to assert the new abstraction
  boundaries and keep websocket listener and reverse fallback runtime
  coverage green

Rationale:
- execution decisions should depend on available capabilities and the
  websocket runtime contract rather than direct connection mode branches
- separating liveness/channel access from execution surfaces makes the
  current launcher code easier to extend toward unified websocket
  execution without a flag day rewrite

ASE-48: paginate ticket runtime transcripts without gaps (#542)

* feat(ticket-detail): paginate runtime transcripts

Summary:
- add a unified ticket run transcript page contract with stable cursors,
  latest-page hydration, and HTTP/service regression coverage
- rebuild ticket run transcript state from stored step/trace entries so
  paging, reconnect backfill, and live SSE updates converge cleanly
- add hidden-history UI, older-page loading, reconnect recovery, and
  reducer/UI real-sample tests for transcript expansion behavior

Rationale:
- fixed-size oldest-window hydration left long-running ticket detail
  transcripts with unrecoverable middle gaps after SSE continued from the
  live tail
- a unified page contract avoids independent step/trace paging drift and
  gives the frontend exact hidden-history metadata for explicit expansion
- replaying transcript state from persisted events keeps assistant text,
  tool cards, and status cards deterministic while older pages prepend

* fix(web): make playwright web server resilient

Summary:
- let Playwright derive its base URL and web server port from env
- enable polling-based file watching for the Playwright Vite web server
- keep the existing e2e mock API path while avoiding watcher EMFILE exits

Rationale:
- Playwright starts Vite in serve mode so the e2e mock middleware is
  available, but native file watchers were exhausting descriptors and
  aborting the web server before tests began
- using polling only for the Playwright web server keeps local defaults
  intact while making CI and low-watch-limit environments stable

Tests:
- PATH=$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH PLAYWRIGHT_WEB_PORT=46964 pnpm -C web run ci

* fix(ci): stream backend test progress in actions

Summary:
- add a helper that keeps backend go test output visible in GitHub
  Actions while preserving quiet local runs
- use the visible runner for the backend full suite and domain/core
  coverage gate
- leave local developer ergonomics unchanged outside Actions

Rationale:
- backend coverage checks were being terminated with exit code 143 on
  the self-hosted runner without any failing test output
- the script only surfaced go test logs on failure, which left the job
  effectively silent during long successful package runs
- streaming package progress in Actions gives the runner continuous
  output without weakening local signal-to-noise

Summary:
- keep backend go test output visible in GitHub Actions
- cap package-level go test concurrency in Actions while preserving an
  env override
- reuse the same parallelism guard for the full suite and coverage gate

Rationale:
- the self-hosted backend job was still being terminated externally even
  after progress output was restored
- the failing runs showed many heavyweight package test binaries active
  at once, which points to runner-side pressure rather than a code test
  failure
- lowering package concurrency in Actions keeps coverage semantics the

commit.message:
fix(resurrect): keep autosave helper alive through a save cycle

Replace the one-shot autosave status renderer with a single autosave command
that acquires the autosave lock for its full lifetime, waits until the next
due save or icon-clear event, and writes status output over time before
exiting. Keep autosave-status as a compatibility alias, but route both names
through the same implementation and update the command-level tests to match.

This avoids spawning a brand new autosave process on every status refresh
while still letting tmux own the helper lifecycle. The lifetime lock now
prevents duplicate helpers from doing work, and each fresh invocation still
picks up the latest interval and icon settings on the next cycle.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

build(release): bundle plugin files in release tarballs

Package each release archive with the tmux-popup-control binary plus
README.md, main.sh, and main.tmux so a downloaded tarball contains the files
needed to install and wire up the plugin without an extra checkout. Rename the
archived executable back to tmux-popup-control inside the tarball so the plugin

artifacts can embed macOS extended attributes that trigger confusing warnings

commit.message:
feat(resurrect): add status-line autosaves and typed restore rows

Add automatic session snapshots that are driven by a tmux status-right helper instead of a long-running daemon.

This wires a new autosave-status subcommand through the main entrypoint, resolves autosave interval, retention, icon, and pane-content settings from tmux options or environment variables, performs due-save checks under a nonblocking lock, updates the default restore target, and prunes only older autosaves. main.tmux also now publishes the resolved binary path so status-right snippets can invoke the plugin without guessing its install location.

The restore-from menu now records save kind in metadata, shows typed rows with the snapshot name first, uses distinct colours for manual and automatic saves, and renders styled rows through the ANSI-aware width path so coloured entries do not pick up bogus trailing ellipses.

commit.message:
[gpu] Fix draw encode blob ownership leaks

hb_gpu_draw_encode() could allocate a fresh output buffer and then
return NULL before transferring ownership to a blob, leaking that
temporary buffer on late encode failures.

The recycled-blob growth path also tried hb_realloc() first and fell
back to hb_malloc() when that failed. Once the new buffer was committed
to the recycled blob, the old recycled buffer could be orphaned.

Free temporary buffers on the remaining late-failure exits and keep
the old recycled buffer alive until a replacement has been committed.
Tighten the GPU API recycle test to encode a larger second outline so
that reused-buffer growth stays covered.

Tested:
- meson test -C build

Assisted-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(preview): render pane cursors from visible pane captures

Add cursor-aware pane previews for pane, window, session, and tree preview paths. The tmux layer now captures visible pane rows, carries cursor coordinates with the preview data, and the UI overlays a white block cursor when the cursor falls inside the rendered preview.

The implementation history on this branch matters to the final shape:
- an initial history-relative capture approach rendered the cursor in the wrong place because it mixed capture output with visible-screen cursor coordinates
- a follow-up attempt using capture-pane with -E -1 truncated previews badly and hid content in sparse panes
- another revision still scrolled sparse single-line panes onto blank trailing rows, making the only content line disappear

The final design fixes those failures by treating the preview as visible pane rows only, slicing around the last meaningful visible row or cursor row, and anchoring pane preview scroll state to the last non-empty content row so sparse panes stay visible.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

runtime: harden shutdown-aware IO and wake paths

Why:
- blocking IO shutdown semantics were inconsistent across the generic IO layer, custom helpers, and coop-owned server loops
- several runtime paths still had production-grade correctness and operability issues around idle waiting, signal wakeup, and sampler publication ordering

What changed:
- add explicit kill-aware blocking wrappers through Handle::WaitKill, macro-generated *Kill helpers, and a documented blocking IO shutdown contract
- add socket-level ShutdownOnKillGuard, convert coop-owned accept and relay loops, and expose kill-aware siblings for custom splice, sendfile, and SSL helpers
- harden HTTP/TLS handlers and examples so shutdown wake behavior is explicit instead of relying on IsKilled checks alone
- fix the idle scheduler wait path, sampler publication ordering, and shutdown signal handling internals
- add targeted IO regressions for accept, recv, poll, splice, and sendfile cancellation paths

fix(release): repair v0.1.1 backend build

Summary:
- fix the skill refinement prompt string so Go compilation no longer breaks
- update OpenAPI and versioned test fixtures from 0.1.0 to 0.1.1
- keep the release branch aligned with the intended v0.1.1 metadata

Rationale:
- the v0.1.1 tag failed to build release binaries because a raw Go string
  contained backticks and produced a syntax error in internal/chat

  tests, which would leave generated artifacts inconsistent with the tag
